title: The Ice Dragon's New Hat
summary: Icecycle the dragon lives a lonely life in his mountain ice castle until his friend Hattie Mouse visits with a special gift: a handcrafted ice hat adorned with jewels and sunblock. Soon, Bearhead the Ice King awakens from a long slumber and discovers his crown is broken. Seeking help, they turn to Miss Plier, a skilled mouse mechanic, to repair the royal headpiece using her trusty tools and restore the king's glory.

highlightQuote.text: How about you being the shadow watcher in my bed on the side of the bed because I always have shadow problems with my yoyo?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually what about me sitting in your lap, Bearhead sitting in my lap?
That's a great idea.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: So you're gonna sit there, I'm gonna sit, lift me up into your lap, and I go Bearhead into my lap.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Oh, that's so cozy. That's the best.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: It's time...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell a story about Icecycle and Hattie Mouse. So Hattie Mouse visiting Icecycle.
Okay. Well...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: One day... there was hat... hat festival, but actually, Daddy...
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: Yeah?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: You tell the Dimitar part, Daddy. Actually the Icecycle should, I'll be telling Hattie, you'll be telling Icecycle.
Once upon a time, Icecycle was sitting all alone in his lonely ice castle high at the top of a mountain...
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Actually he should be flying down to Hattie's mouse, to Hattie's workshop.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And he decided since he was so lonely that he should visit his friend Hattie.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, you're gonna be telling Hattie, actually ding dong ding dong Icecycle.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Who is it?
Hattie. I brought a jewel hat for you!
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: You did? Jewels are my favorite. I don't even have any hats.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Actually I bring you an ice hat. I made you an ice... I made a hat made out of ice.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: Wow, that sounds like a hard task. And it's big enough to fit on my huge dragon head? Let me see. And what did the ice hat look like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A circle with a sunblock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And did it have decorations on it?
Some jewel decorations of course.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And were they colored or white?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Colored. Pink and purple and silver and gold.
My goodness! This is the most beautiful gift anyone has ever given me. Thank you, Hattie.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: And he put it on and he was so happy and proud to wear his ice hat. And he says, Hattie, I'm so glad you visited me. I'm a little lonely up here at the top of the mountain.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually Bearhead's the Ice King, right Bearhead?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Bearhead said, uh, hello everyone. Am I the Ice King? I've been asleep for many years and I don't even know if I'm still the king. Am I wearing a crown?
No. But I can make you one with pictures of bears of course. So we got a shiny one in the attic but it's a bit broke.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: I see. Well...
But you can still wear it.
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: And Bearhead said, I'd like to be the Ice King... wait, what kind of king was he? The Ice King?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Ice King.
I'd like to be the Ice King once again, but I will need some help getting a new crown.
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, there's a new one in the attic but it's a bit broken. I think you'll call Mistress or Hattie. Actually Hattie makes clothing and Mistress fixes cars.
Oh, so there's no one who can fix my crown?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, actually there's Miss Plier the mouse who can fix your crown.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And where does Miss Plier live?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, like across the street from Dimitar's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, but Dimitar lives far, far away on the Circular Island. How can we get there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Actually, actually she lives right across from Hattie's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Oh.
So Plier's gonna come to fix your crown right now.
transcript.speaker: Izzy
Dad
transcript.text: She's coming here? Oh, thank goodness.
And Icecycle the dragon said...
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Actually ding dong Bearhead.
And Bearhead said, who is it?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Um, Mrs. Plier. Oh, come on in. I heard someone gave me an invitation that said your crown was broken, so I would like to fix it for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, please, please do fix it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well I'll use just my... I'll just use my wrench. It's easy. And I'll just use my screwdriver too. Screw, screw, screw, plier, actually screw, screw, screw, wrench, wrench, wrench, wrench, wrench. All fixed.
Wow. Will you put it on my head? said Bearhead.
transcript.speaker: Dad

transcript.text: Oh, actually we gotta go get the one in the attic tomorrow. But I'm getting a bit tired, Bearhead. How about you being the shadow watcher in my bed on the side of the bed because I always have shadow problems with my yoyo?
transcript.speaker: Izzy

transcript.text: I see. Okay, Bearhead's getting in the bed. Is that the end of Stars and Stories?
transcript.speaker: Dad
Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah. That's the end.

characters.name: Icicle
characters.description: A titanic and ancient white ice dragon, this towering companion to Dimitar commands the skies with freezing breath and vast, powerful wings. From a shimmering ice palace atop a remote mountain peak, he presides over his crystalline domain with a timeless and frigid majesty, sustained exclusively by a diet of frozen fish.

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Bearhead
characters.description: Awakened from an eon of glacial slumber, the Ice King is a massive bear with a disproportionately large head crowned in frost. Though he rules a kingdom of frozen silence, he is a master of rhymes and rhythmic riddles, emerging from his lethargy to traverse the snows and deliver heartfelt Valentine’s surprises with a whimsy that defies his biting winter chill.

characters.name: Miss Plier
characters.description: A mouse mechanic who is skilled at fixing broken crowns using tools like wrenches and screwdrivers.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

places.name: Ice Castle
places.description: Perched high in the snowy peaks, Icecycle’s lonely fortress is a majestic bastion of solitude constructed from massive blocks of ice. Its jagged, frozen surfaces are meticulously buffed with specialized polishing kits, transforming the mountaintop stronghold into a brilliant, diamond-like spectacle.

places.name: Hattie's Workshop
places.description: Tucked within the cozy hollow of a weathered tree, Hattie Mouse’s dwelling serves as a busy, imaginative atelier and social hub where the air hums with the rhythmic snip of scissors. Here, amidst a cheerful clutter of measuring tapes, wet pigments, and scavenged shells, she crafts her signature starry hats from an eclectic mix of fur and feathers. The workshop is bathed in the earthy radiance of her fungal store—a brilliant collection of red, orange, and yellow mushrooms—illuminating the haunting indigo witch painting where Hattie first encountered the squeaking worm. It is in this storied corner, beneath the witch's gaze, that she recently unearthed a mysterious message hidden alongside a cache of sparkly green clover stickers.

places.name: Circular Island
places.description: A perfectly circular landmass rising from the waters of the distant reaches, this remote sanctuary serves as both the private residence of Dimitar and the hallowed grounds of the school.

places.name: The Attic
places.description: At the spaceship’s highest point—a vital hub for testing critical systems and the original home of Oscar Mouse before his move—lies a dusty alcove within Isabella’s home; there, in a vault Seeker once discovered, a fractured crown still glimmers with a stubborn, broken brilliance.
